zh.Lienden, Buren, Gelderland, Netherlands: 荷蘭蓋爾德藍德布倫連登

zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnes Van Este Van Wachtendonck於1150年1月8日出生在荷蘭努-布拉班特洛 奧普 展德時，她的父親Diederik van Este van Wachtendonk31歲，母親Agnes van Loon20歲。她於1165年與Floris Van Lijnden在荷蘭蓋爾德藍德連登結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1203年1月7日在荷蘭蓋爾德藍德連登享年52歲，埋葬在荷蘭蓋爾德藍德連登。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Agnes Van Este Van Wachtendonck于1150年1月8日出生在荷兰努-布拉班特洛 奧普 展德时，她的父亲Diederik van Este van Wachtendonk31岁，母亲Agnes van Loon20岁。她于1165年与Floris Van Lijnden在荷兰蓋爾德藍德連登结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1203年1月7日在荷兰蓋爾德藍德連登享年52岁，埋葬在荷兰蓋爾德藍德連登。

nameMeanings.name: Van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Este
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: East
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who came from the east or who lived on the eastern side of a settlement. Compare   Estes  .
Americanized form (translation into English) of North German Ost and Swedish Öst.

Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes Van Este Van Wachtendonck was born on 8 January 1150, in Loon op Zand, North Brabant, Netherlands, her father, Diederik van Este van Wachtendonk, was 31 and her mother, Agnes van Loon, was 20. She married Floris Van Lijnden in 1165, in Lienden, Gelderland, Netherlands. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 7 January 1203, in Lienden, Gelderland, Netherlands, at the age of 52, and was buried in Lienden, Gelderland, Netherlands.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Agnes Van Este Van Wachtendonck est née le 8 janvier 1150, à Loon op Zand, Brabant-septentrional, Pays-Bas, son père, Diederik van Este van Wachtendonk, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Agnes van Loon, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé Floris Van Lijnden en 1165, à Lienden, Gueldre, Pays-Bas. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 7 janvier 1203, à Lienden, Gueldre, Pays-Bas, à l’âge de 52 ans, et a été enterrée à Lienden, Gueldre, Pays-Bas.

de.lifeSummary: Als Agnes Van Este Van Wachtendonck am 8. Januar 1150 in Loon op Zand, Nord-Brabant, Niederlande geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Diederik van Este van Wachtendonk 31 und ihre Mutter Agnes van Loon 20. Sie hat 1165 in Lienden, Geldern, Niederlande Floris Van Lijnden geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb am 7. Januar 1203 im Alter von 52 Jahren in Lienden, Geldern, Niederlande und wurde in Lienden, Geldern, Niederlande begraben.

it.Loon op Zand, North Brabant, Netherlands: Loon op Zand, Brabante settentrionale, Paesi Bassi
it.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes Van Este Van Wachtendonck è nata il il 8 gennaio 1150 nel Loon op Zand, Brabante settentrionale, Paesi Bassi, sua padre, Diederik van Este van Wachtendonk, aveva 31 anni e sua madre, Agnes van Loon, aveva 20 anni. Lei sposato Floris Van Lijnden nel 1165, nel Lienden, Gelderland, Paesi Bassi. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 7 gennaio 1203, nel Lienden, Gelderland, Paesi Bassi, all'età di 52 anni, e fu sepolta a Lienden, Gelderland, Paesi Bassi.

ja.Lienden, Gelderland, Netherlands: オランダジェルダーランドリーンデン
ja.lifeSummary: Agnes Van Este Van Wachtendonckが1150年1月8日にオランダノオルド-ブラバントロオン オップ ザンドで生まれた時，父Diederik van Este van Wachtendonkは31歳，母Agnes van Loonは20歳であった。1165年にオランダジェルダーランドリーンデンでFloris Van Lijndenと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1203年1月7日にオランダジェルダーランドリーンデンで52歳で死去し，オランダジェルダーランドリーンデンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes Van Este Van Wachtendonck(이)가 1150년 1월 8일 네덜란드 노르드-브라반트 루 프 자드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Diederik van Este van Wachtendonk(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agnes van Loon(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 1165년 네덜란드 글델랜드 리든에서 Floris Van Lijnden(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1203년 1월 7일 네덜란드 글델랜드 리든에서 52세의 나이로 사망했으며 네덜란드 글델랜드 리든에 묻혔다.

pt.Loon op Zand, North Brabant, Netherlands: Loon op Zand, Brabante do Norte, Países Baixos
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes Van Este Van Wachtendonck nasceu em 8 de janeiro de 1150, em Loon op Zand, Brabante do Norte, Países Baixos, seu pai, Diederik van Este van Wachtendonk, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Agnes van Loon, tinha 20 anos. Ela casou-se com Floris Van Lijnden em 1165, em Lienden, Gheldria, Países Baixos. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 7 de janeiro de 1203, em Lienden, Gheldria, Países Baixos, com 52 anos, e foi sepultada em Lienden, Gheldria, Países Baixos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnes Van Este Van Wachtendonck родилась 8 января 1150 г. в местности лун оп занд, нурд-брабант, Нидерланды, её отцу по имени Diederik van Este van Wachtendonk было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Agnes van Loon, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Floris Van Lijnden в 1165 г. в местности лиенден, джелдирланд, Нидерланды. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла 7 января 1203 г. в местности лиенден, джелдирланд, Нидерланды в возрасте 52 лет и была похоронена в местности лиенден, джелдирланд, Нидерланды.

es.8 Jan 1150–7 Jan 1203: 8 de enero de 1150–7 de enero de 1203
es.Loon op Zand, North Brabant, Netherlands: Loon op Zand, Brabante del Norte, Países Bajos
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Agnes Van Este Van Wachtendonck nació el 8 de enero de 1150, en Loon op Zand, Brabante del Norte, Países Bajos, su papá, Diederik van Este van Wachtendonk, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Agnes van Loon, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Floris Van Lijnden en 1165, en Lienden, Gelderland, Países Bajos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 7 de enero de 1203, en Lienden, Gelderland, Países Bajos, a la edad de 52 años, y fue sepultada en Lienden, Gelderland, Países Bajos.
